title: Integrity тАФ Unexpected UPDATE/DELETE on PHI Tables (Clarity/Caboodle)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648)
spl:
index=mssql sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h action_class="DML"
| where match(lower(object_name),"pat|patient|phi|epic") AND match(lower(statement),"update|delete")
| eval is_bulk=if(match(statement,"(?i)where\s+1=1|truncate|delete\s+from"),1,0)
| where is_bulk=1 OR (match(lower(application_name),"microsoft sql server management studio") AND _time < relative_time(now(),"@d+18h"))
| stats count by session_server_principal_name, object_name, application_name
| sort - count
description: Alerts on bulk or off-hours SQL changes to tables holding patient demographics or clinical facts so improper alteration of ePHI is detected and investigated.
value: Alerts on bulk or off-hours SQL changes to tables holding patient demographics or clinical facts so improper alteration of ePHI is detected and investigated.
grandmaExplanation: We warn you about bulk or off-hours SQL changes to tables holding patient demographics or clinical facts so improper alteration of ePHI is detected and investigated тАФ so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Enable SQL Server Audit to SIEM; (2) map ETL service accounts to allow list; (3) pair with change tickets from ServiceNow; (4) retain immutable copy to WORM storage if required.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
тАв Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648).
тАв Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=mssql` `sourcetype=mssql:audit` (`statement`, `schema_name`, `object_name`, `application_name`, `session_server_principal_name`).
тАв For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
(1) Enable SQL Server Audit to SIEM; (2) map ETL service accounts to allow list; (3) pair with change tickets from ServiceNow; (4) retain immutable copy to WORM storage if required.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mssql sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h action_class="DML"
| where match(lower(object_name),"pat|patient|phi|epic") AND match(lower(statement),"update|delete")
| eval is_bulk=if(match(statement,"(?i)where\s+1=1|truncate|delete\s+from"),1,0)
| where is_bulk=1 OR (match(lower(application_name),"microsoft sql server management studio") AND _time < relative_time(now(),"@d+18h"))
| stats count by session_server_principal_name, object_name, application_name
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Integrity тАФ Unexpected UPDATE/DELETE on PHI Tables (Clarity/Caboodle) (┬з164.312(c)(1))** тАФ Alerts on bulk or off-hours SQL changes to tables holding patient demographics or clinical facts so improper alteration of ePHI is detected and investigated.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mssql` `sourcetype=mssql:audit` (`statement`, `schema_name`, `object_name`, `application_name`, `session_server_principal_name`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedтАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mssql; **sourcetype**: mssql:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

тАв Scopes the data: index=mssql, sourcetype="mssql:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
тАв Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(object_name),"pat|patient|phi|epic") AND match(lower(statement),"update|delete")` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
тАв `eval` defines or adjusts **is_bulk** тАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
тАв Filters the current rows with `where is_bulk=1 OR (match(lower(application_name),"microsoft sql server management studio") AND _time < relative_time(now()тАж` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
тАв `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by session_server_principal_name, object_name, application_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
тАв Orders rows with `sort` тАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 тАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (principal, object, count), Timeline (bulk ops).

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern тАФ correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
